mission.title: Install and Configure Kind (Kubernetes in Docker)
mission.description: Kind (Kubernetes in Docker) allows you to run local Kubernetes clusters using Docker containers. It's primarily designed for testing Kubernetes itself but can also be used for local development or CI. The key trade-off is that it runs entirely in Docker, which may not replicate all aspects of a production environment.

steps.title: Install Kind
steps.description:
This step installs Kind using the pre-built binary for your operating system.

```bash

[ $(uname -m) = x86_64 ] && curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-$(uname)-amd64

[ $(uname -m) = aarch64 ] && curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-$(uname)-arm64

chmod +x ./kind

sudo mv ./kind /usr/local/bin/kind

```

> For AMD64 / x86_64 on Linux
> For ARM64 on Linux
> Make the Kind binary executable
> Move Kind to a directory in your PATH

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster using Kind with the default configuration.

```bash

kind create cluster --image kindest/node:v1.31.0

```

> Creates a cluster with Kubernetes version 1.31.0

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step ensures that your kubeconfig is set up to use the newly created Kind cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=$(kind get kubeconfig-path --name=

```

> Set the KUBECONFIG environment variable to point to the Kind cluster

steps.title: Verify the Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks that the nodes in your Kind cluster are running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Verify that the nodes are in the Ready state

uninstall.title: Remove the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the Kind cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that any data stored in the cluster will be lost.

```bash

kind delete cluster

```

> Delete the Kind cluster

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kind
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of any important data. The upgrade process involves deleting the existing cluster and creating a new one with the updated version.

```bash

kind delete cluster

kind create cluster --image kindest/node:v1.31.0

kubectl get nodes

```

> Delete the existing cluster
> Create a new cluster with the desired version
> Verify that the new cluster is running

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kind: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kind binary is not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Ensure that /usr/local/bin is in your PATH or move the Kind binary to a directory that is in your PATH.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: failed to start docker container'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Docker is not running or not properly configured.

**Fix:**
Start Docker and ensure it is running correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubelet not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes node failed to start properly.

**Fix:**
Check Docker logs for the Kind node container and troubleshoot any issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster creation process timed out.

**Fix:**
Increase the timeout duration or check your Docker resource limits.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31.0, improved error messages during cluster creation.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get nodes' showing the nodes in the Ready state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure Docker is installed and running on your machine.
